summary: AI Preventive Healthcare Intelligence Platform - a web-based dashboard featuring health risk prediction (using patient vitals like age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose), chest X-ray analysis, health tips, and prediction history tracking.
architectural_notes: Multi-page navigation: Risk Predictor, X-Ray Analysis, Dashboard, History
Frontend: web-based (originally Streamlit code provided, adapt to standard web stack)
ML inference should be simulated on frontend since no backend model files are available
known_issues: Build has failed multiple times; simplify scope to core health risk input form and dashboard
App deployment failing - user reports app is not deployed at all

existing_features.title: Build a multi-page frontend application with a sidebar navigation containing four pages: Risk Predictor, X-Ray Analysis, Dashboard, and History.

Implement a Health Risk Predictor page with input fields for Age (20–100), BMI (10.0–50.0), Blood Pressure (80–200 mmHg), Glucose (50–300 mg/dL), Hemoglobin (5–20 g/dL), Cholesterol (100–400 mg/dL), Gender (Male/Female), and Smoker (checkbox). On form submission, compute a simulated risk score using weighted thresholds (BMI < 25, BP < 120, glucose < 110, cholesterol < 200, hemoglobin ≥ 12 reduce risk; smoking increases risk) and display a color-coded gauge chart (green = low < 40%, orange = moderate 40–75%, red = high > 75%) with a risk level label.

Implement an X-Ray Analysis page that accepts an image file upload (JPG/PNG). Since ML model inference is not available, simulate a classification result: randomly assign 'Normal' or 'Pneumonia' with a simulated confidence score between 60–99%. Display the uploaded image, the prediction label, confidence percentage, and a bar chart comparing Normal vs Pneumonia probabilities. Show a disclaimer that this is not a medical diagnosis.

Implement a Health Dashboard page that displays all past risk prediction entries in a table (columns: Age, BMI, Blood Pressure, Glucose, Hemoglobin, Cholesterol, Risk Score, Risk Level). Include a button to export the table data as a CSV file download.
Implement a Prediction History page that lists all past risk predictor results and X-ray analysis results in separate sections. Each entry should show the input values and the computed outcome.

Apply a clean, clinical yet modern visual theme: dark navy/teal color palette with crisp white cards, subtle green accents for healthy metrics, amber for moderate risk, and red for high risk. Use a professional medical-grade typography style with clear data hierarchy. Layout should use a fixed sidebar for navigation and a main content area with card-based sections.

Update `frontend/src/pages/RiskPredictor.tsx` to call `useAddRecord` on successful form submission, passing all form values plus the computed risk score and risk level, so every prediction is persisted to the backend.

Audit and fix all TypeScript/build errors in the frontend that are causing the deployment pipeline to fail. Ensure `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` correctly maps the backend Candid interface for `addRecord` and `getAllRecords`, that the `HealthRecord` TypeScript type exactly matches all backend fields, and that all pages (`RiskPredictor.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, `History.tsx`) compile without type errors.

Audit and fix all TypeScript compilation errors in the frontend so the build pipeline completes successfully. Specifically: ensure `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` correctly maps the backend Candid interface for `addRecord` and `getAllRecords`; ensure the `HealthRecord` TypeScript type exactly matches all backend fields; and ensure `RiskPredictor.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, `History.tsx`, and `XRayAnalysis.tsx` all compile without type errors. The `npm run build` command must succeed with zero errors.

new_feature_requests.summary: Health risk predictor with inputs for age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, and smoking status
Chest X-ray image upload and classifier showing Normal vs Pneumonia prediction with confidence score
Health dashboard showing historical predictions and exportable results
Prediction history page showing past risk and X-ray results
Risk gauge visualization (low/moderate/high) with color-coded risk levels

Store all prediction history (risk assessments and X-ray results) in the Motoko backend as persistent stable storage. Expose query and update endpoints to save a risk assessment record and retrieve all records.

Ensure the Motoko backend actor uses stable storage (stable var or StableHashMap) for all health records so that data persists across canister upgrades and page refreshes. The actor must expose: (1) an update function `addRecord` that accepts all risk predictor fields (age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, smoker, computed risk score, risk level, and a timestamp) and stores the record, and (2) a query function `getAllRecords` that returns all stored records in reverse-chronological order.

Update the `useAddRecord` and `useGetAllRecords` React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to correctly map the backend's `addRecord` and `getAllRecords` Candid interfaces. Ensure the `HealthRecord` TypeScript type includes all fields returned by the backend (age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, smoker, riskScore, riskLevel, timestamp).

Update `frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` and `frontend/src/pages/History.tsx` to load records via `useGetAllRecords` from the backend instead of any local in-memory state. Both pages should display live persisted data that survives page refreshes.

Audit and fix all compilation errors in `backend/main.mo` and `backend/migration.mo` that are causing deployment failures. Ensure the Motoko actor compiles cleanly: stable storage declarations are correct, all imported modules resolve, and the `addRecord`/`getAllRecords`/`getRecordById` endpoints have valid type signatures matching the HealthRecord shape (age, bmi, bloodPressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, smoker, riskScore, riskLevel, timestamp).

Audit and fix all compilation errors in `backend/main.mo` and `backend/migration.mo` so the Motoko actor compiles cleanly. Verify that: stable storage declarations are syntactically correct, all imported modules resolve, and the `addRecord`, `getAllRecords`, and `getRecordById` endpoints have valid type signatures matching the HealthRecord shape (age, bmi, bloodPressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, smoker, riskScore, riskLevel, timestamp). The backend must compile without errors before deployment.
